items.name: schema-builder-core
items.title: Schema Builder — Core
items.description: Shared core for the schema-builder block family: config provider, schema selectors + block-local store, GraphQL read hooks, the databases list UI, skeletons, and the diagram SVG substrate. A library the schema-builder area blocks build on — not mounted on its own.
items.docs:
## Setup

1. Mount `blocks-runtime` once at your app root (installed automatically) with the namespaces this block uses, and set their endpoints:

```tsx
<BlocksRuntime
  namespaces={['schema-builder', 'auth']}
  getToken={() => tokenManager.getAccessToken()}
>
  {children}
</BlocksRuntime>
```

- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SCHEMA_BUILDER_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT` (owner lookups)

2. This block imports its hooks from your generated SDKs (`@/generated/schema-builder`, `@/generated/auth`). Ensure they are generated. The declared operations ship to `~/.constructive/blocks/schema-builder-core.requires.json` for the SDK-check tool.

items.name: schema-builder-fields
items.title: Schema Builder — Structure
items.description: Structure tab for the schema-builder family: the table editor — add/edit/remove columns, types, defaults, nullability, and primary-key/unique constraints.

items.name: schema-builder-policies
items.title: Schema Builder — Policies
items.description: Policies tab for the schema-builder family: row-level security policy editing, table grants, and the create-table-with-policies flow.
items.docs:
## Setup

1. Mount `blocks-runtime` once at your app root (installed automatically) with the namespaces this block uses, and set their endpoints:

```tsx
<BlocksRuntime
  namespaces={['schema-builder', 'modules', 'admin']}
  getToken={() => tokenManager.getAccessToken()}
>
  {children}
</BlocksRuntime>
```

- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SCHEMA_BUILDER_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_MODULES_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT` (permission catalog for the Policies tab)

2. This block imports its hooks from your generated SDKs (`@/generated/schema-builder`, `@/generated/modules`, `@/generated/admin`). Ensure they are generated. The declared operations ship to `~/.constructive/blocks/schema-builder-policies.requires.json` for the SDK-check tool.

items.name: schema-builder
items.title: Schema Builder
items.description: Composer shell for the schema-builder family: wires the core library and the Structure, Relationships, Indexes, and Policies area blocks into one self-contained Postgres schema editor with card-stack navigation. No QueryClient or auth provider of its own.
items.docs:
## Setup

1. Mount `blocks-runtime` once at your app root (installed automatically) with the namespaces this block uses, and set their endpoints:

```tsx
<BlocksRuntime
  namespaces={['schema-builder', 'modules', 'auth', 'admin']}
  getToken={() => tokenManager.getAccessToken()}
>
  {children}
</BlocksRuntime>
```

- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SCHEMA_BUILDER_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_MODULES_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT`
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT` (owner lookups)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT` (permission catalog for the Policies tab)

2. This block imports its hooks from your generated SDKs (`@/generated/schema-builder`, `@/generated/modules`, `@/generated/auth`, `@/generated/admin`). Ensure they are generated. The declared operations ship to `~/.constructive/blocks/schema-builder.requires.json` for the SDK-check tool.
